site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner ladder strategy, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 20 tips, market-aware ladder strategy, and Mark Hughes Foundation ledger discipline.

ticker: round 20 tips are broncos sharks roosters raiders warriors bulldogs titans and dolphins
market favourites are leader-behaviour signals not automatic bets
prince_of_penrith is chasing a 5 point gap to second before finals
controlled differentials are broncos over panthers and titans over sea eagles
dolphins over cowboys is a coin flip lean that needs final market and dearden confirmation
charity ledger stays at 3 and 2 with $31.50 invested $32.03 returned and plus $0.53 settled profit
no round 20 charity bet is locked without fresh odds stake and explicit confirmation

featurePanels.title: Ladder Target
items: User-supplied Round 19 ladder screenshot shows Prince_of_Penrith 3rd on 194 points.
Second place is Kristy W13 on 199, so the actionable target is a 5-point swing before finals.
First place NourilM is on 211, a 17-point gap; that is a longer chase and should not drive reckless weekly tactics.
Round 20 keeps the same plan: match the obvious safe picks, then choose controlled splits where team news creates a real edge.

featurePanels.title: Market Read
items: Exact live betting prices were not recorded in this update, so market bands are marked as leader-behaviour estimates rather than confirmed odds.
The top two are likely to protect position by taking favourite-heavy cards.
Heavy or clear favourites should usually be matched unless late mail gives us a concrete reason to split.
The two main controlled Round 20 splits are Broncos over Panthers and Titans over Sea Eagles.
Dolphins over Cowboys is the lowest-confidence coin-flip lean and should be rechecked if Tom Dearden is formally promoted from the reserves.

featurePanels.title: Round 20 Tips
items: Broncos over Panthers - controlled split, high risk but backed by Brisbane's returning spine and middle.
Sharks over Knights - favourite match, strong home lean with Hynes and Nikora edge.
Roosters over Storm - favourite match, because Melbourne are without Cameron Munster and Jahrome Hughes.
Raiders over Rabbitohs - favourite match at GIO with Ethan Strange and Hudson Young back.
Warriors over Dragons - protect the likely favourite with Capewell, Barnett and Dallin Watene-Zelezniak back.
Bulldogs over Wests Tigers - protect, but monitor Stephen Crichton, Viliame Kikau and Api Koroisau late mail.
Titans over Sea Eagles - controlled split at Cbus after the Titans nearly pinched Storm and Manly lost middle depth.
Dolphins over Cowboys - coin-flip home lean with the Dolphins' outside backs returning.

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Recheck Panthers v Broncos after final 24-hour cut and any late spine changes.
Do not ignore Melbourne's Roosters record, but missing Munster and Hughes is the deciding selection factor.
Bulldogs v Tigers can move fast if Kikau or Api Koroisau is promoted.
Dolphins v Cowboys needs final Dearden confirmation and a market check before lockout.
No Round 20 charity bet is locked. The card is for tipping-comp strategy first.

betCard.title: Round 20 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Round runs Thursday 16 July to Sunday 19 July 2026
why: This update is a tipping-comp strategy card, not a confirmed charity-bet slip.
Exact market odds and stake were not recorded during the update.
A charity bet only becomes official after explicit user confirmation before kickoff.
risks: Using ladder-chase pressure as betting motivation would mix two ledgers that must stay separate.
Round 20 has several late-mail moving parts, especially Broncos, Storm, Bulldogs, Tigers, Dolphins and Cowboys.
Any future charity play still needs recorded odds, stake, match, and explicit confirmation before kickoff.

watchlist: Prince_of_Penrith needs a 5-point swing to catch 2nd, not a miracle card to catch 1st immediately.
Final market prices must be checked before lockout because this update uses market behaviour estimates, not captured odds.
Broncos over Panthers is a controlled split and should be reviewed if Brisbane make late spine changes.
Roosters over Storm depends heavily on Storm missing Munster and Hughes.
Bulldogs over Tigers should be rechecked if Stephen Crichton, Viliame Kikau or Api Koroisau is promoted.
Dolphins over Cowboys is the lowest-confidence tip and should be rechecked if Dearden is confirmed to start.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: The top-two chase now includes market modelling
lines: Round 20 is Women in League Round and runs from Thursday 16 July to Sunday 19 July 2026.
The Round 20 draw has eight games and Parramatta on the bye.
Prince_of_Penrith is still working from the supplied ladder target: 3rd on 194 points and 5 points behind 2nd.
The two leaders are expected to lean favourite-heavy, so the app now treats market favourite status as a proxy for leader-safe behaviour.
The goal is controlled edge, not full-card chaos.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: No charity bet locked for Round 20
lines: The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
No Round 20 charity result should be added without a pre-recorded stake, price and explicit charity-bet confirmation.
The market is being used here for tipping-comp psychology and leader modelling.
Any actual charity play needs a separate confirmation before kickoff.

sections.title: Round 20 Tips
sections.heading: Recommended card for Prince_of_Penrith
lines: Panthers v Broncos - Broncos, controlled split. Brisbane regain Reece Walsh, Ezra Mam, Adam Reynolds, Payne Haas and Patrick Carrigan.
Sharks v Knights - Sharks, favourite match. Cronulla regain Briton Nikora and start Addin Fonua-Blake, while Newcastle have injury reshuffle around Marzhew, Hopwood and McEwen.
Roosters v Storm - Roosters, favourite match. Melbourne are without Cameron Munster and Jahrome Hughes; Roosters regain Sam Walker and Reece Robson.
Raiders v Rabbitohs - Raiders, favourite match. Canberra regain Ethan Strange and Hudson Young at GIO.
Warriors v Dragons - Warriors, protect. Kurt Capewell, Mitch Barnett and Dallin Watene-Zelezniak return.
Bulldogs v Wests Tigers - Bulldogs, protect. Canterbury are at home and Jacob Preston is back, but watch Crichton/Kikau/Api late mail.
Titans v Sea Eagles - Titans, controlled split. Gold Coast nearly upset Storm and Manly are without Ben Trbojevic and Corey Waddell.
Dolphins v Cowboys - Dolphins, coin-flip lean. The Dolphins regain Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow, Jack Bostock and Selwyn Cobbo, while Cowboys still need final Tom Dearden confirmation.

sections.title: Round 20 Fixtures
sections.heading: Round 20 schedule
lines: Panthers v Broncos - Thu 16 Jul, 7:50pm at CommBank Stadium.
Sharks v Knights - Fri 17 Jul, 6:00pm at Ocean Protect Stadium.
Roosters v Storm - Fri 17 Jul, 8:00pm at Allianz Stadium.
Raiders v Rabbitohs - Sat 18 Jul, 3:00pm at GIO Stadium.
Warriors v Dragons - Sat 18 Jul, 5:35pm AEST, 7:35pm local at Go Media Stadium.
Bulldogs v Wests Tigers - Sat 18 Jul, 7:35pm at Accor Stadium.
Titans v Sea Eagles - Sun 19 Jul, 2:00pm at Cbus Super Stadium.
Dolphins v Cowboys - Sun 19 Jul, 4:05pm at Suncorp Stadium.
Bye: Parramatta Eels.

sections.title: Top-Two Plan
sections.heading: How this card tries to gain without blowing up
lines: Match clear favourites in Sharks, Raiders, Warriors and Bulldogs so Prince_of_Penrith does not donate points to the field.
Use Broncos as the main early split because Brisbane's named side is dramatically stronger than Round 18.
Use Titans as the Sunday controlled split because the market and leaders may favour Manly reputation, but Gold Coast have home ground and better team continuity.
Keep Dolphins as a flexible coin-flip lean; switch to Cowboys if final market and Dearden confirmation strongly move North Queensland.
Do not force extra underdogs. A 5-point gap can be closed with two strong split wins across the run home.

sections.title: Late Mail
sections.heading: Selection notes driving the card
lines: Broncos: Walsh, Mam, Reynolds, Haas, Piakura and Carrigan are back; Billy Walters will not face Penrith.
Panthers: Cleary, Martin, Yeo, Casey McLean and Isaiah Papali'i all return.
Storm: Munster and Hughes are both sidelined, so Tyran Wishart and Trent Toelau form a new halves pairing.
Roosters: Tedesco and Lindsay Collins remain sidelined, but Sam Walker and Reece Robson return.
Warriors: Capewell, Barnett and Watene-Zelezniak return; Charnze Nicoll-Klokstad is listed in the reserves.
Bulldogs and Tigers: Crichton, Kikau and Koroisau are reserve-list watch items.
Cowboys: Dearden is expected to be called up from the reserves if he gets through training.

footerNotes: Built from the Codex-maintained data workflow and updated for Round 20 on Wednesday 15 July 2026.
Prince_of_Penrith ladder context came from the user-supplied Round 19 overall ladder screenshot: 3rd, 194 points, 5 behind 2nd.
Round 20 charity bet status is no bet locked, so the Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains 3-2 and +$0.53.
Exact market odds were not recorded in this update; market notes are leader-behaviour estimates until a final price check is added.
